items.title: The Smartest Dividend Stocks to Buy With $500 Right Now
items.author: Adria Cimino
items.description: The article recommends three Dividend Kings - Johnson & Johnson, Abbott Laboratories, and Coca-Cola - as smart dividend buys for investors with $500 or less. It highlights the companies' long track records of dividend growth, strong free cash flow, and diversified business models.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article highlights Johnson & Johnson's over 60-year history of increasing dividends, its strong free cash flow, and its growth potential in the pharmaceutical and medical device businesses after spinning off its consumer health business.
The article praises Abbott's more than 50-year history of dividend growth, its diversified healthcare business, and its strong pipeline of new product launches.
The article emphasizes Coca-Cola's over 60-year dividend growth track record, its strong free cash flow, and its diverse portfolio of beverage brands that have helped the company grow over time.

items.title: Eli Lilly Is Putting Dividend Growth Stocks to Shame With Yet Another Huge Increase to Its Payout
items.author: The Motley Fool
items.description: Eli Lilly has been increasing its dividend at a high rate, with a 15% increase for the seventh consecutive year. The company has a bright future with promising drugs in its portfolio, and its dividend growth potential makes it an attractive long-term investment despite its low current yield.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article highlights Eli Lilly's impressive dividend growth, with a 15% increase for seven consecutive years, and its promising drug pipeline, including weight loss and diabetes treatments as well as an Alzheimer's treatment. This suggests the company has a bright future and is a solid long-term investment.
The article compares Eli Lilly's dividend growth to that of Johnson & Johnson, a healthcare stock with a longer dividend growth streak, but does not provide a specific sentiment on Johnson & Johnson.

The article also compares Eli Lilly's dividend growth to that of Becton, Dickinson, another healthcare stock with a long dividend growth streak, but does not provide a specific sentiment on Becton, Dickinson.

The article uses Walgreens Boots Alliance as an example of a company that slashed its dividend despite a long dividend growth streak, highlighting that a company's financial health is more important than its dividend growth history.

items.title: Johnson & Johnson submits application to the European Medicines Agency seeking approval of a new indication for IMBRUVICA® (ibrutinib) in adult patients with previously untreated mantle cell lymphoma (MCL) who are eligible for autologous stem cell transplant
items.author: N/A
items.description: Johnson & Johnson has submitted an application to the European Medicines Agency seeking approval for a new indication of its drug IMBRUVICA (ibrutinib) in the treatment of previously untreated mantle cell lymphoma in adult patients eligible for autologous stem cell transplant. The application is supported by data from the TRIANGLE study, which showed improved efficacy with ibrutinib-based regimens compared to the current standard of care.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article highlights Johnson & Johnson's efforts to expand the approved indications for its drug IMBRUVICA (ibrutinib) in the treatment of mantle cell lymphoma, which is seen as a positive development for the company.
